DISCOVERY 612, NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL IS GRANTED. NO OPPOSITION HAS BEEN FILED. DEFENDANT'S FAILURE TO RESPOND WAS A MISUSE OF THE DISCOVERY PROCESS. DEFENDANT IS ORDERED TO RESPOND WITHIN 15 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. SANCTIONS ARE AWARDED IN THE AMOUNT OF $650, TO BE PAID WITHIN 30 DAYS OF NOTICE OF THIS ORDER. IF ANY COUNSEL WISHES TO APPEAR IN PERSON OR BY TELEPHONE ON THIS TENTATIVE RULING, GIVE NOTICE AS REQUIRED BY CRC 324 AND NOTIFY THE CLERK OF THE DISCOVERY DEPARTMENT. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT

DISCOVERY, 612, NO APPEARANCE. TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED. PLAINTIFF'S MOTION TO COMPEL AND REQUEST FOR SANCTIONS IS ORDERED OFF CALENDAR AS IT WAS NOT TIMELY FILED. C.C.P. SECTION 1005(B) REQUIRES THAT A MOTION BE FILED AS WELL AS SERVED AT LEAST 21 DAYS PRIOR TO THE HEARING. WHILE THE PROOF OF SERVICE IS DATED SEPTEMBER 21, 2004, THE MOTION WAS NOT FILED UNTIL OCTOBER 5, 2004. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
